step_by_step_thinking: I agree with agent1's assessment. One additional point worth noting is that while the GRID reduces scattered radiation, it does so by absorbing part of the primary x-ray beam. To maintain adequate penetration through tissues, an increased exposure time may be required, thereby increasing overall patient dose. This trade-off between improved image clarity via reduced scatter versus higher effective dose must be carefully considered in clinical practice. Thus, my final answer remains consistent with Option A.
